title: GEP Analysis of BM CD34+/lin- Cells in CML Patients
author: Alessandra Trojani
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.yncfvaw
materials: 4Immediately after, label the cells with Anti-Biotin Microbeads.
Counting of BM CD34+/lin- cells and cell cryopreservation
8Resuspend the cells in 50 µl of RNAlater (Thermo Fisher Scientific, Milano, Italy) and store at -20°C.
11Test BM CD34+/lin- cells by standard FISH.
13Cohybridize samples to XL BCR/ABL1 plus Translocation/Dual Fusion Probe (MetaSystems, Milan, Italy) on ThermoBrite Statspin Model (Leica Biosystems, US).
19Perform RNA extraction using MagMAX 96 Total RNA Isolation Kit (Thermo Fisher Scientific) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
20Measure quality and yield of the extracted RNA using Nanodrop (Thermo Fisher Scientific).

steps.title: Automated isolation of bone marrow CD34+/lin- using immunomagnetic beads
steps.instruction: Automated isolation of bone marrow CD34+/lin- using immunomagnetic beads

steps.title: 1Isolate Mononuclear cells (MNCs) from the...
steps.instruction: 1Isolate Mononuclear cells (MNCs) from the bone marrow (BM) blood samples (range, 1-25 ml) using Ficoll density gradient centrifugation at 800 rpm for 20 minutes.

steps.title: 2Immediately after, select the BM CD34+/lin-...
steps.instruction: 2Immediately after, select the BM CD34+/lin- cells using Diamond CD34 Isolation kit and autoMACs Pro separator (Miltenyi Biotech) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: 3Label MNCs with a mix of...
steps.instruction: 3Label MNCs with a mix of biotin-conjugated antibodies against lineage-specific antigens.

steps.title: 5Select the lineage-negative stem and progenitor...
steps.instruction: 5Select the lineage-negative stem and progenitor cells by the depletion of the magnetically labeled cells. BM CD34+/lin- cells were obtained from the lineage-negative stem and progenitor cells using CD34 Microbeads (Miltenyi Biotec) and following the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: 6Dilute the cell samples in Trypan...
steps.instruction: 6Dilute the cell samples in Trypan blue and count the cells using hemocytometer.

steps.title: 7Calculate the number of cells for...
steps.instruction: 7Calculate the number of cells for each sample using the following formula: Total cells counted x dilution factor/n°squares x 104 cells / ml x 2 ml cell suspension.

steps.title: 9Preserve the BM CD34+/lin- cells in...
steps.instruction: 9Preserve the BM CD34+/lin- cells in 50 µl of RNAlater when the number of the cell count was equal to 10,000 or more and the cells were stored at -20°C.

steps.title: 10If the number of BM CD34+/lin-...
steps.instruction: 10If the number of BM CD34+/lin- cells was less than 10,000, resuspend the cells in 10 µl of Prelude direct Lysis module (NuGEN, AC Leek, The Netherlands) and immediately after, store the cells at -80°C.

steps.title: 12Perform FISH analysis of a small...
steps.instruction: 12Perform FISH analysis of a small sample of selected CD34+/lin- cells (containing at least 103 cells fixed in Carnoy’s solution) using standard methods.

steps.title: 14Perform FISH analysis using fluorescence microscope...
steps.instruction: 14Perform FISH analysis using fluorescence microscope Axioskop 2 (Carl Zeiss Microimaging GmbH, Göttingen, Germany), equipped with a UV 100-W lamp (Osram, Augsburg, Germany), ProgRes MF CCD camera (Jenoptik AG, Jena, Germany), and ISIS System Software (MetaSystems Hard & Software, Althlussheim, Germany).

steps.title: 15Count, at least, 200 interphase nuclei...
steps.instruction: 15Count, at least, 200 interphase nuclei from each suitable specimen (optimum: 300 nuclei).

steps.title: 16Read each available interphase nucleus even in sub-optimal specimens
steps.instruction: 16Read each available interphase nucleus even in sub-optimal specimens.

steps.title: 17Obtain results according to the International...
steps.instruction: 17Obtain results according to the International System for Human Cytogenetic Nomenclature (ISCN) [24].

steps.title: 18Isolate total RNA from the BM...
steps.instruction: 18Isolate total RNA from the BM CD34+/lin- cells of CML patients previously stored in RNAlater.

steps.title: 21Do not perform RNA extraction of...
steps.instruction: 21Do not perform RNA extraction of the BM CD34+/lin- cells stored in Prelude direct Lysis module (NuGEN) because of the too low number of cells and perform the RNA processing starting from the cells.

steps.title: 22Prepare cDNA starting from the previously...
steps.instruction: 22Prepare cDNA starting from the previously extracted RNA (50 ng) using Ovation Pico WTA System V2 kit (NuGEN) and Encore Biotin Module Kit (NuGEN) following the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: 23Hybridize cDNA to Affymetrix HTA 2
steps.instruction: 23Hybridize cDNA to Affymetrix HTA 2.0 using the GeneChip platform (Affymetrix).

steps.title: 24Use Affymetrix GeneChip Scanner 3000 according...
steps.instruction: 24Use Affymetrix GeneChip Scanner 3000 according to the manufacturer’s instructions to scan the signals.

steps.title: 25Prepare cDNA starting from 3 µl...
steps.instruction: 25Prepare cDNA starting from 3 µl of the BM CD34+/lin- cells resuspended in Prelude direct Lysis module (NuGEN), using Ovation One Direct System kit followed by Encore Biotin Module Kit (NuGEN) adhering to the manufacturer’s instructions.

steps.title: 26Hybridize cDNA to Affymetrix HTA 2
steps.instruction: 26Hybridize cDNA to Affymetrix HTA 2.0 using the GeneChip platform (Affymetrix).

steps.title: 27The preprocessing of microarray raw data...
steps.instruction: 27The preprocessing of microarray raw data was performed using R software version 3.4.2. The Affymetrix HTA 2.0 probes were initially summarized into probe sets specific for a given gene using function RMA of R package oligo, downloaded from Bioconductor repository version 3.4. Principal component analysis (PCA) has been performed using prcomp function of package stats version 3.4.2.

steps.title: 28MvA plots were generated using custom scripts
steps.instruction: 28MvA plots were generated using custom scripts. MvA plots show the relationship among the average log intensity of the gene expression (A value) and the log of intensity ratio (M value) between two samples.

steps.title: 29PCA and MvA plots were examined...
steps.instruction: 29PCA and MvA plots were examined before and after microarray preprocessing as quality checking procedure. PCA plots revealed the presence of batch effects due to the different protocols used for performing RNA extraction and GEP experiments. Batch effects have been corrected using function ComBat of R package sva.

steps.title: 30MvA plots showed the presence of...
steps.instruction: 30MvA plots showed the presence of bias in the distribution of intensities among samples, then data have been normalized using function normalize.quantiles of R package preprocessCore.

steps.title: 31The differential expression analysis was performed...
steps.instruction: 31The differential expression analysis was performed on the samples at 12 months vs. diagnosis using the two-classes SAM test, implemented in the homonym function in R package samr. Benjamini-Hochberg procedure was applied to control the False Discovery Rate (FDR) and a cut-off value of 0.05 was applied to select for significant differential expression.

steps.title: 32Functional clustering was performed on significant...
steps.instruction: 32Functional clustering was performed on significant differentially expressed genes using online tool DAVID (https://david.ncifcrf.gov/), to classify them into functional groups based on their annotation term co-occurrence.

steps.title: 33Use for the analysis protein coding...
steps.instruction: 33Use for the analysis protein coding genes having a unique EntrezID in the “Affymetrix NetAffx” annotation were used (HTA 2.0 Transcript Cluster Annotations, Release 36, 7/6/16).
